behavior_areas.summary: Detached executable launch now waits up to 1.25 seconds for short-lived child processes to materialize side effects before returning.
missing_later_contour_tests: prove the settle window matters by using a short-lived detached child that writes a marker shortly after start, then assert immediately after command return without an extra polling loop
prove detached launch remains bounded and does not become an unbounded wait
prove return shape and bounded claim scope stay unchanged

behavior_areas.summary: When lock_acquired is true, routing_change_observed no longer requires selected_backend_ids membership locally; active placement and policy proof still apply, and selected-backend participation is expected to be owned by caller postflight checks.
existing_tests: tests/test_cli.py stage-advance success cases around stage 15 and stage 20
missing_later_contour_tests: prove lock_acquired run_promote succeeds when selected_backend_ids does not yet include the promoted backend and caller postflight owns that proof
prove direct accounts promote without lock_acquired still fails or rolls back when selected_backend_ids does not verify the backend
prove stage advance postflight catches selected-backend participation when local promote delegates that check

independent_inspector.key_finding: Diff is confined to detached-client settling and lock-acquired promotion verification predicate.

owner_decision_options.when_to_choose: Owner believes both behavior areas may be valid and wants tests/contract review before accepting them.
Owner wants an exact revert of the runtime tail in a separate approved execution contour.
Owner wants launch/client timing and rollout/stage promotion semantics decided separately.
Owner wants no mutation yet. This branch remains blocked for UI backfill while runtime.py is dirty.
